# Prototype Build Schedule — Shell A (84 m²)

Last updated: 2026-05-30
Location: Lagos, Algarve, Portugal
Target: Q3 2026 (August–September)

---

## Overview

| Phase | Weeks | Focus | Key Deliverable |
|-------|-------|-------|-----------------|
| **Pre-build** | Weeks 1–4 | Site prep, materials, permits, team | Site ready, materials delivered, team assembled |
| **Build** | Weeks 5–6 | Foundation, frame, panels, roof | Weathertight shell complete |
| **Systems** | Weeks 7–8 | Electrics, plumbing, insulation, interior | Liveable space ready |
| **Monitoring** | Week 9+ | Sensor network, documentation, handover | 12-month monitoring begins |

**Total build time:** 14 days of active construction (spread across 4 weeks with drying/curing time)
**Team size:** 4–6 people (2 experienced, 2–4 trainees/learners)
**Weather dependency:** Low — all work is under cover or can be done in light rain

---

## Pre-Build Phase (Weeks 1–4)

### Week 1: Site Acquisition + Legal
- [ ] Confirm build site (200 m² minimum, flat, Lagos area)
- [ ] Get landowner agreement (rental, loan, or municipal partnership)
- [ ] Submit simplified building permit application (if required for <100 m² temporary structure)
- [ ] Check with Câmara Municipal de Lagos — Urbanismo department
- [ ] Verify no planning restrictions (agricultural zone, protected area, etc.)

**Deliverable:** Signed site agreement + permit confirmation

### Week 2: Materials Sourcing + Budget
- [ ] Order EPAL pallets: 180–220 units (budget €8–12 each = €1,440–2,640)
- [ ] Source I-joists: 40–50 units (budget €15–25 each = €600–1,250)
- [ ] Order OSB sheathing: 90 sheets 12mm (budget €12–18 each = €1,080–1,620)
- [ ] Order cement-board sheathing: 70 sheets (budget €15–20 each = €1,050–1,400)
- [ ] Order mineral wool insulation: 140 m² (budget €8–12/m² = €1,120–1,680)
- [ ] Order membrane + roofing: 110 m² (budget €5–8/m² = €550–880)
- [ ] Order screws, brackets, fixings: €300–500
- [ ] Order electrics: cable, sockets, LED lights, breaker box (€500–800)
- [ ] Order plumbing: PEX pipe, fittings, water tank (€400–600)
- [ ] Order windows: 5–6 units (€150–300 each = €750–1,800)
- [ ] Order door: 2 units (€100–200 each = €200–400)
- [ ] Source ground screws or screw piles for foundation: 24–28 units (€25–40 each = €600–1,120)
- [ ] Arrange delivery to site

**Deliverable:** All materials ordered and paid for; delivery scheduled for Week 4

### Week 3: Team Assembly + Training
- [ ] Recruit 4–6 build team members (mix of experienced + learners)
- [ ] Hold 2-hour safety briefing (power tools, lifting, working at height)
- [ ] Review build manual with team (docs/build/build_protocol_shell_a.md)
- [ ] Assign roles: lead builder, panel assembler, roof team, electrics, plumbing, documentation
- [ ] Arrange tools: circular saw, drill/driver, level, tape measure, clamps, ladder/scaffold
- [ ] Book accommodation for team if needed (if site is rural)

**Deliverable:** Team assembled, trained, and ready

### Week 4: Site Prep + Delivery
- [ ] Clear and level site
- [ ] Mark foundation points (ground screw positions)
- [ ] Set up materials storage area (under tarp if no weather protection)
- [ ] Set up tool station, first aid kit, water station
- [ ] Deliver all materials to site
- [ ] Take "before" photos and video for documentation
- [ ] Install ground screws / screw piles (if using)
- [ ] Install temporary foundation pads (if using concrete pads)

**Deliverable:** Site ready, materials on-site, foundation in place

---

## Build Phase (Weeks 5–6) — 14 Active Days

### Day 1: Floor Frame
- [ ] Lay out pallet floor grid (7×12 m = ~90 pallets for 84 m²)
- [ ] Level and shim pallets on foundation
- [ ] Secure pallets to each other with timber brackets
- [ ] Install edge beams (I-joists or timber beams)
- [ ] Install subfloor (OSB sheathing on top of pallet grid)

**End of Day 1:** Solid, level floor platform ready

### Day 2–3: Wall Panels — Preparation
- [ ] Cut I-joists to length for wall frames (4× wall sections: 2 long, 2 short)
- [ ] Cut OSB sheathing to size for wall panels
- [ ] Pre-assemble wall panels on ground (frame + sheathing)
- [ ] Install mineral wool insulation in wall cavities
- [ ] Number and label each panel for assembly sequence

**End of Day 3:** All wall panels prefabricated and ready to lift

### Day 4: Wall Panels — Erection
- [ ] Lift and secure first wall panel (corner)
- [ ] Brace temporarily with timber props
- [ ] Lift second wall panel, connect at corner
- [ ] Continue until all 4 walls are standing
- [ ] Check plumb and level at each corner
- [ ] Secure panels to floor frame with brackets
- [ ] Install temporary diagonal bracing across walls

**End of Day 4:** All 4 walls standing, braced, connected to floor

### Day 5: Roof Structure
- [ ] Install roof beams (I-joists or timber beams) across walls
- [ ] Install roof pallet grid (lightweight pallet layer on beams)
- [ ] Install roof sheathing (OSB or cement-board)
- [ ] Install roof membrane (waterproof breathable layer)
- [ ] Install roofing material (corrugated metal, bitumen sheets, or tiles)
- [ ] Install overhangs and fascia boards

**End of Day 5:** Weathertight roof complete

### Day 6: Windows + Doors + Sealing
- [ ] Cut window openings in wall panels (if not pre-cut)
- [ ] Install window frames and windows
- [ ] Install door frame and door
- [ ] Seal all joints with expanding foam or tape
- [ ] Install external membrane (rainscreen if using cement-board)
- [ ] Install external cladding (if using — can be delayed)

**End of Day 6:** Building is weathertight — rain and wind protected

### Day 7: Electrics
- [ ] Run cable from external connection point (or solar battery)
- [ ] Install breaker box / distribution board
- [ ] Run cable to socket locations, light switches, light fixtures
- [ ] Install sockets, switches, and LED lights
- [ ] Test all circuits with multimeter
- [ ] Connect to power source (temporary or permanent)

**End of Day 7:** Power available inside the building

### Day 8: Plumbing
- [ ] Install water tank (if off-grid) or connect to mains
- [ ] Run PEX pipe to kitchen sink and shower location
- [ ] Install drain lines (if connecting to septic or greywater system)
- [ ] Install sink and basic fixtures
- [ ] Test water pressure and drainage

**End of Day 8:** Water available inside the building

### Day 9: Insulation + Interior
- [ ] Fill any gaps in wall insulation with loose mineral wool
- [ ] Install internal wall lining (OSB, plywood, or plasterboard)
- [ ] Install ceiling insulation and lining
- [ ] Install floor finish (OSB, plywood, or vinyl)
- [ ] Seal all internal joints with tape or caulk

**End of Day 9:** Thermally insulated and finished interior

### Day 10: Systems + Testing
- [ ] Install any additional systems: solar panel, battery, inverter, compost toilet, etc.
- [ ] Test all electrics under load
- [ ] Test water system for leaks
- [ ] Check for drafts and seal any gaps
- [ ] Take thermal camera photos (if available) to identify cold bridges

**End of Day 10:** All systems operational and tested

### Day 11–12: Interior Fit-Out
- [ ] Install kitchen unit (basic: counter, sink, storage)
- [ ] Install sleeping area (platform bed or fold-out)
- [ ] Install seating / dining area
- [ ] Install storage solutions
- [ ] Paint or seal internal surfaces
- [ ] Install curtains or blinds
- [ ] Basic furnishing: mattress, chairs, table, cooking equipment

### Day 13–14: Handover + Documentation
- [ ] Complete build manual with as-built notes and photos
- [ ] Record all material costs with receipts
- [ ] Record all labour hours by person and task
- [ ] Install sensor network (temperature, humidity, energy, structural)
- [ ] Take "after" photos and video
- [ ] Hold team debrief and lessons learned session
- [ ] Celebrate

**End of Day 14:** Shell A complete and documented. 12-month monitoring begins.

---

## Systems Phase (Weeks 7–8)

### Week 7: Interior Fit-Out
- [ ] Install kitchen unit (basic: counter, sink, storage)
- [ ] Install sleeping area (platform bed or fold-out)
- [ ] Install seating / dining area
- [ ] Install storage solutions
- [ ] Paint or seal internal surfaces
- [ ] Install curtains or blinds
- [ ] Basic furnishing: mattress, chairs, table, cooking equipment

### Week 8: Exterior + Landscaping
- [ ] Install external cladding (if not done during build)
- [ ] Paint or seal external surfaces
- [ ] Install guttering and drainage
- [ ] Create basic outdoor area: gravel path, seating, shade
- [ ] Plant windbreak or shade trees (if site allows)
- [ ] Install security measures (simple lock, maybe camera)

---

## Monitoring Phase (Week 9+)

### Week 9: Sensor Calibration + Baseline
- [ ] Install temperature sensors (interior, exterior, wall cavity)
- [ ] Install humidity sensors (interior, exterior, wall cavity)
- [ ] Install energy monitor (if on grid) or solar production monitor (if off-grid)
- [ ] Install structural deflection sensors (if available)
- [ ] Set up data logging system (Raspberry Pi or Arduino + cloud upload)
- [ ] Calibrate all sensors against known reference
- [ ] Record baseline readings
- [ ] Set up dashboard for real-time monitoring

### Ongoing: Monthly Checks
- [ ] Download and review sensor data monthly
- [ ] Visual inspection of structure for cracks, settlement, moisture
- [ ] Photograph any changes or issues
- [ ] Log all maintenance actions
- [ ] Publish quarterly data reports on GitHub / website

---

## Budget Recap

| Category | Estimated Cost |
|----------|---------------|
| Materials (pallets, timber, sheathing, insulation, roofing) | €10,000–15,000 |
| Windows, doors, electrics, plumbing | €2,000–3,500 |
| Foundation (ground screws or pads) | €600–1,120 |
| Tools (if not owned) | €500–1,000 |
| Sensors + monitoring system | €300–500 |
| Labour (if paid) | €2,000–5,000 |
| Interior fit-out + furnishing | €3,000–6,000 |
| Contingency (15%) | €2,700–4,800 |
| **Total** | **€19,100–35,920** |

Note: The €20,000 shell target is achievable with volunteer labour and bulk sourcing. The €50,000 finished home includes kitchen, bathroom, solar, and all interior fit-out.

---

## Contingency Planning

| Risk | Probability | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|------------|
| Rain delays | Medium | Tarp covers, work under roof once walls are up |
| Material shortage | Low | Order 10% extra; have backup supplier |
| Team member drops out | Medium | Recruit 6 people for 4 spots |
| Tool failure | Low | Bring backup drill, spare blades |
| Foundation issues | Low | Use ground screws (adjustable) instead of concrete |
| Pallet quality variation | Medium | Inspect all pallets before delivery; reject damaged ones |
| Permit delay | Medium | Start permit process 6 weeks early |

---

## Success Criteria

The prototype is considered successful if:
- [ ] Built within 14 active days
- [ ] Total cost under €20,000 (materials only)
- [ ] Finished home under €50,000 (all-inclusive)
- [ ] Weathertight and thermally comfortable (no drafts, no condensation)
- [ ] All electrics and plumbing working safely
- [ ] Sensor data flowing continuously
- [ ] No structural deformation or settlement after 3 months
- [ ] Build manual is complete enough for a stranger to replicate

---
